task_completion.description: Verify that the GIMP application is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the target image is loaded into GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A portrait photo of a person (or a landscape photo as a fallback) is visible on the GIMP canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigates to the Cubism filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' menu is open, specifically navigating through the 'Artistic' submenu towards 'Cubism', or a search dialog for 'Cubism' is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Cubism filter dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Cubism' dialog box is visible on the screen, showing settings like tile size, tile saturation, or background color.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Cubism effect has been successfully applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the main canvas has visibly changed to have a fragmented, geometric, tile-like cubist appearance, confirming the filter was applied.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being used to process the image.

integrity.description: Verify the original image was actually modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being modified should match the initially loaded Unsplash photo, rather than a pre-computed cubist image being pasted or opened.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI using automated scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent running Python-Fu or Script-Fu scripts in the console to instantly apply the effect without interacting with the filter dialogs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is a real photo from Unsplash (typically a 800x1200 portrait, though a landscape fallback exists). The correct application of the Cubism effect in GIMP will introduce distinct geometric patterns, increase the edge density significantly (by at least 1.5x), and alter at least 20% of the image pixels. Look for a clear fragmented, tile-like visual change on the canvas to confirm the effect was applied.
